primaryTopics.label: Education and Learning
primaryTopics.description: School, university, study methods, assessment and language learning.

primaryTopics.label: Technology and Media
primaryTopics.description: Mass media, information technology, inventions and communication impact.

primaryTopics.label: Work, Business and Economy
primaryTopics.description: Workplace policy, marketing, jobs, wages and economic tradeoffs.

primaryTopics.label: Society, Law and Policy
primaryTopics.description: Legal rules, social responsibility, rights and behavior change.

primaryTopics.label: Environment and Global Issues
primaryTopics.description: Climate change and broad global challenges.

primaryTopics.label: City, Infrastructure and Housing
primaryTopics.description: Urban development, transport, buildings and living choices.

promptTypes.label: Agree or Disagree
promptTypes.description: Take a clear position and support it with reasons and examples.

promptTypes.label: Discuss Both Views and Opinion
promptTypes.description: Present both sides, then give your own position.

promptTypes.label: Advantages and Disadvantages
promptTypes.description: Discuss pros and cons and provide your judgment.

promptTypes.label: Cause, Problem and Solution
promptTypes.description: Explain causes or key problems and suggest practical solutions.

promptTypes.label: Responsibility Allocation
promptTypes.description: Decide who should take the main responsibility and explain why.

promptTypes.label: Policy Rule with Example
promptTypes.description: Comment on a rule/policy and provide concrete examples or alternatives.

promptTypes.label: Choice Preference with Reasons
promptTypes.description: Choose one side or option and justify your choice.

promptTypes.label: Describe and Evaluate
promptTypes.description: Describe a case/item and evaluate its impact.

promptTypes.label: Issue Analysis and Opinion
promptTypes.description: Analyze an issue from key angles and provide a clear opinion.

difficultyScale.label: Easy
difficultyScale.description: Direct wording and simple argument structure.

difficultyScale.label: Hard
difficultyScale.description: Multi-part prompt or abstract reasoning needed.

editorialRules: Use promptText as the canonical question text in all matching and history features.
Use displayTitle/sourceNumberLabel as compact display metadata only.
Prefer simple high-school level words in templates and sentence banks.
Keep primary topic taxonomy stable; do not add sparse top-level topics in phase1.
